detailed_analysis: collision_blocker.py is a small (155-line), well-factored Layer-2 use-case helper that supplies the CollisionBlockerMixin to DiscoveryWalker. It is structurally sound: every function is well under the 40-line limit, nesting never exceeds 4, names are two-word/role-bearing, and it honours the Dependency Rule (imports only inward — agentic_tool_spec, rendering, shared_keyed_map_io, state, sync_types — and references no concrete tool name). The architecture's I-4/NFR-13 structured-logging invariant is largely respected: collision log lines name pair_id, target path and slot. Two broad `except Exception` blocks exist; neither is a classic C3 silent-swallow — both log via logging.exception/logging.error and deliberately fail closed (treat as collision / block). The strongest residual concerns are (a) a borderline NFR-13 completeness gap: the plan-failure branch logs only pair_id, not the agentic_tool or path AC-10 calls for; (b) a recurring three-field parameter clump (targets / target_display / blocked accumulators with verbose tuple-keyed dict types) shared across three methods — a data-clump / primitive-obsession smell; (c) a bare module-level logging call rather than a module logger. No CRITICAL findings, no secrets, no injection, no God Class. Security axis is clean (no input boundary handled here beyond read_slots, which is delegated). Verdict: AUDIT_PASS.

findings.problem: The pair (targets, target_display), both keyed by the verbose primitive tuple type `tuple[str, str | None]`, co-occurs across three method signatures (block_target_collisions locals, _collect_targets_and_detect_collisions params lines 94-95, _detect_multi_pair_collisions params lines 142-143). They are two facets of one concept (a collision-key registry). Fowler Data Clumps / Primitive Obsession: a small accumulator type (e.g. a CollisionTargetRegistry with the tuple key abstracted) would remove the repeated 2-field threading and the repeated raw tuple-dict type annotations.

findings.variant: Broad catch (defensible fail-closed) with incomplete structured context
findings.problem: This is NOT a silent swallow — it logs via logging.exception and fails closed by blocking the pair, which is the safe choice and is consistent with US-03 AC-10 (a malformed/unplannable artifact is excluded and does not block other pairs). The residual concern is NFR-13/I-4 completeness: the structured line names only pair_id, while NFR-13 asks for the agentic_tool and US-03 AC-10 expects the agentic_tool and path of the offending artifact. `info` carries per-tool/path detail that is not surfaced. Catching bare Exception (rather than the specific planning/IO error type) also widens the net beyond the intended failure mode.

All six log calls go through the root logging facade rather than a module-scoped logger (`logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)`). Every other invariant (I-4) call site is structured, but using the root logger forfeits per-module level control and the module name in records, weakening the structured-error contract's traceability. Style/maintainability hint, not a defect.

The two arms of the slot-None / slot-set branch differ only by a fixed message prefix and pass identical arguments. A single log call with a computed prefix would remove the duplication. Minor; the explicit branching is readable and arguably intentional, so this is an INFO-level DRY hint only.

verdict_summary: Clean, well-factored Layer-2 collision-blocking mixin; one WARNING data-clump and three INFO hints, no CRITICAL/MAJOR findings, Dependency Rule and NFR-13 substantially upheld.
